test_cases.failure_message:
Timed out after 1200.001s.
KubeadmControlPlane object self-hosted/self-hosted-1db3jr-control-plane was not initialized in time
The function passed to Eventually failed at /home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/test/e2e/common.go:265 with:
KubeadmControlPlane is not yet initialized
Expected
    <bool>: false
to be true
test_cases.failure_body:
[FAILED] Timed out after 1200.001s.
KubeadmControlPlane object self-hosted/self-hosted-1db3jr-control-plane was not initialized in time
The function passed to Eventually failed at /home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/test/e2e/common.go:265 with:
KubeadmControlPlane is not yet initialized
Expected
    <bool>: false
to be true
In [It] at: /home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/test/e2e/common.go:266 @ 06/08/26 16:43:48.304

ai_summary.summary: KubeadmControlPlane initialization failed due to Azure VM allocation failure

ai_analysis.root_cause: The KubeadmControlPlane object self-hosted/self-hosted-1db3jr-control-plane failed to initialize because its corresponding Azure VM (self-hosted-1db3jr-control-plane-ccvrb) could not be provisioned. The CAPZ controller log at artifacts/clusters/bootstrap/logs/capz-system/capz-controller-manager/capz-controller-manager-576f4948dd-g7q8z/manager.log shows repeated 'ZonalAllocationFailed' errors for this VM (lines 3420-3421, 3519-3520). The Azure error message states: 'Allocation failed. We do not have sufficient capacity for the requested VM size in this zone.' This is a transient infrastructure issue where Azure cannot allocate the requested VM size in the specified zone. The CAPI controller manager log at artifacts/clusters/bootstrap/logs/capi-system/capi-controller-manager/capi-controller-manager-7fcc6ff995-8s7jq/manager.log confirms the KubeadmControlPlane remained uninitialized, with messages indicating it was 'provisioning' and 'not yet initialized' (e.g., line 265).
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Retry the test. This is a transient Azure infrastructure capacity issue that should resolve on its own or with a different zone/VM size selection in subsequent runs.

test_cases.failure_body:
[FAILED] Timed out after 1200.001s.
KubeadmControlPlane object md-remediation-rs9no3/md-remediation-opqhn1-control-plane was not initialized in time
The function passed to Eventually failed at /home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/test/e2e/common.go:265 with:
KubeadmControlPlane is not yet initialized
Expected
    <bool>: false
to be true
In [It] at: /home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/test/e2e/common.go:266 @ 06/08/26 16:43:48.336

ai_summary.summary: Control plane VM failed to provision due to Azure capacity constraints

ai_analysis.root_cause: The KubeadmControlPlane object md-remediation-rs9no3/md-remediation-opqhn1-control-plane failed to initialize because its underlying Azure VM (md-remediation-opqhn1-control-plane-vdw5r) could not be provisioned. The CAPZ controller manager log shows repeated 'ZonalAllocationFailed' errors when trying to create the VM, indicating Azure does not have sufficient capacity for the requested VM size (Standard_B2s) in the specified zone (CanadaCentral/zone 1). The Azure activity log confirms this with a 'ResourceOperationFailure' containing the 'ZonalAllocationFailed' error message. This is a transient infrastructure issue, not a bug in the test or code.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: The failure is due to temporary Azure capacity constraints in the CanadaCentral region. A retry of the test with a different region or VM size would be the appropriate action, but this cannot be determined from the logs alone.

The function passed to Eventually failed at /home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/test/e2e/common.go:265 with:

test_cases.failure_body:
[FAILED] Timed out after 1200.001s.
KubeadmControlPlane object md-scale-iv0eu9/md-scale-pn9aoz-control-plane was not initialized in time
The function passed to Eventually failed at /home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/test/e2e/common.go:265 with:
KubeadmControlPlane is not yet initialized
Expected
    <bool>: false
to be true
In [It] at: /home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/test/e2e/common.go:266 @ 06/08/26 16:43:48.366

ai_summary.summary: Control plane failed to initialize due to Azure VM allocation failure in a specific zone

ai_analysis.root_cause: The KubeadmControlPlane for cluster md-scale-pn9aoz failed to initialize because its control plane machine (md-scale-pn9aoz-control-plane-t6bnm) could not be provisioned in Azure. The Azure activity logs and CAPZ controller logs show repeated 'ZonalAllocationFailed' errors for this VM. For example, the CAPZ controller log at artifacts/clusters/bootstrap/logs/capz-system/capz-controller-manager/capz-controller-manager-576f4948dd-g7q8z/manager.log line 4887 shows: 'ERROR CODE: ZonalAllocationFailed' with the message 'Allocation failed. We do not have sufficient capacity for the requested VM size in this zone'. This is an infrastructure-level capacity issue, not a bug in the code. The CAPI controller logs also show it was waiting for the control plane to become ready, with messages like 'Scale up on hold because KubeadmControlPlane md-scale-iv0eu9/md-scale-pn9aoz-control-plane is provisioning' in artifacts/clusters/bootstrap/logs/capi-system/capi-controller-manager/capi-controller-manager-7fcc6ff995-8s7jq/manager.log. The test timed out because the control plane never became available due to this provisioning failure.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: This is an Azure infrastructure capacity issue that cannot be fixed by changes to the test or CAPZ code. The specific VM size in the selected zone (CanadaCentral zone 1) is unavailable. A potential operational fix would be to retry the test or configure the template to use a different zone or VM size, but this is not a code remediation.

ai_summary.summary: KubeadmControlPlane failed to initialize due to Azure VM allocation failure

ai_analysis.root_cause: The KubeadmControlPlane object md-rollout-v4d210/md-rollout-ylcxvm-control-plane failed to initialize because its underlying Azure VM (md-rollout-ylcxvm-control-plane-6m5lb) could not be provisioned. The CAPZ controller log shows repeated 'ZonalAllocationFailed' errors from Azure when trying to create the VM, indicating insufficient capacity for the requested VM size in the specified zone (CanadaCentral). The error message from Azure states: 'Allocation failed. We do not have sufficient capacity for the requested VM size in this zone.' This is a transient infrastructure issue, not a bug in the code under test.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: The failure is due to temporary Azure capacity constraints in the CanadaCentral zone. A retry of the test with a different zone or VM size, or waiting for capacity to become available, would be the appropriate action.
